proved: The workspace exposes one optional remote review caller for OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, and xAI while local inference remains the stated default.

Each call reads the current semantic draft, binds a SHA-256 snapshot identity, supplies one editable model route and question, and cannot forge disclosure categories, approval, provenance, or credentials.
Rust still derives disclosure and provenance, requires the native Send once decision before vault/network access, normalizes failure, and exposes cancellation by exact call identity.
Provider output remains a non-mutating local review artifact; it is not silently inserted into the collaborative draft.

A headless 1280x720 browser pass found the provider/model/question controls visible, uniquely labeled, contained within the scenario rail, and free of horizontal overflow; browser-only Tauri-backend warnings were unrelated and pre-existing.
P-25 is implemented_unverified rather than verified because browser interaction and a real opt-in provider run remain unproved.
notProved: A live API credential, paid request, provider policy behavior, output quality, or cost estimate.
Streaming output in the product UI, tool calls, structured output, retries, or adversarial multi-provider execution.
Installed-app keyboard/pointer/accessibility behavior or cancellation timing against each live provider.
Persistence, sharing, acceptance/rejection, or provenance export for the returned review artifact.
